answer:
The calendar of a given year repeats when the number of days between the two years is a multiple of 7 and the leap-year pattern matches. The year 2028 is a leap year, so the next year with the same calendar must also be a leap year and have the same starting day. Checking the options, 2056 is a leap year exactly 28 years later, which is a multiple of 7 days, so the calendar repeats.

\boxed{C}
